prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for formaldehyde (H₂C=O) were computed at the DFT level using tier 'standard' (B3LYP/def2-TZVP) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Exact four-center integrals (no density fitting). Geometry taken from the supplied .xyz file (single-point, no re-optimization). Mulliken partial-charge scheme. N±1 states: cation (charge +1, mult 2), anion (charge −1, mult 2).

Headline results per atom [O, C, H, H]:
  f⁺  = [0.46814, 0.70839, −0.08827, −0.08826]
  f⁻  = [0.46742, 0.11244,  0.21008,  0.21006]
  f⁰  = [0.46778, 0.41042,  0.06090,  0.06090]
  Δf  = [+0.00071, +0.59595, −0.29835, −0.29832]

Most electrophilic atom: C (atom 2), f⁺ = 0.70839 (headline value).
Most nucleophilic atom: O (atom 1), f⁻ = 0.46742.

Integrity: status=ok, trustworthy=true. Fukui charge conservation: sum(f⁺)=1.0000, sum(f⁻)=1.0000 — both passed. No warnings raised by the engine. Output JSON: formaldehyde_fukui_dft.json. Plot: .../agent_call_01_plot.png.
